meaning: adj.自由意志的,自愿的,任意的
examples: You may, however, present as a freewill offering an ox or a sheep that is deformed or stunted, but it will not be accepted in fulfillment of a vow.
All the Israelite men and women who were willing brought to the Lord freewill offerings for all the work the Lord through Moses had commanded them to do.
Maybe you spend your free time managing a flower bed in the park – why not put a little wooden freewill donation box out there for people to drop a coin in?
More than a century later, the same flicker of freewill sparked the War for Independence and fired up a new nation.
Mr. SCHACTMAN: As one guy I really respect who's a lieutenant colonel pointed out, it's like you can't predict who's going to win the Iowa caucuses, and now, you're going to replicate freewill.
